text: Deep learning is a relatively new field of research that has led to the development of generative models as an alternative solution for the scarcity of time series data in real life applications. Deep learning models require massive amount of data in order to be trained. Time series data from real life applications as financial and clinical time series suffer from the data scarcity problem, in addition to the class imbalance and high sensitivity to noise issues. Hence, data augmentation (DA) has become a very important technique to assist the deep learning models by generating sufficient training data to improve the performance of the model and to enhance the robustness against different types of noise. In this paper, we provide a detailed survey on the recent works in time series data augmentation by introducing a taxonomy that classifies the augmentation methods into two main subcategories: Transformation based methods, such as jittering, scaling and permutation, and Generative models including Variational Autoencoders (VAEs) and Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs), in addition to Alignment-based methods like Dynamic Time Warping (DTW) barycenter averaging. We also analyzed each method highlighting the advantages and drawbacks of each approach, in addition to discussing the difficulties and challenges facing the data augmentation research field, such as the instability of the GANs, as well as providing the performance evaluation and the corresponding metrics that are used in the field of time series data augmentation. Our ultimate goal is to summarize the state of the art of time series data augmentation in an attempt to recommend to the time series modeling community the most efficient data augmentation techniques in order to increase the lead time accuracy and to utilize synthetic data in enriching the real data.
explanation: The passage is cohesive and uses standard survey-language with domain terms, which can come from either a human or an AI. The neutral, generic framing and absence of a distinctive author voice slightly favor AI-generation, but it remains plausible as human-authored.
